toolUseResult.originalFileContents:
name: Command Battle Arena - Data-Driven Death Matches
# Where similar commands fight with METRICS as weapons

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 12 * * 4'  # Thunderdome Thursdays at noon
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      battle_type:
        description: 'Type of battle'
        required: true
        default: 'category_clash'
        type: choice
        options:
          - category_clash     # Similar commands fight
          - performance_duel   # Speed vs Speed
          - efficiency_war     # Token cost battle
          - complexity_combat  # Simplicity contest
      
      contestants:
        description: 'Specific commands to battle (comma-separated) or "auto"'
        required: false
        default: 'auto'

jobs:
  prepare-arena:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      battle_id: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}
      contestants: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.contestants }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Battle Arena
        id: setup
        run: |
          # Create battle infrastructure
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/rounds
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/results
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/evidence
          
          echo "battle_id=$(date +%Y%m%d)-$(uuidgen | cut -c1-8)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "timestamp=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d\ %H:%M:%S\ UTC)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Select Combatants
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the BATTLE MATCHMAKER with a PhD in Command Combat.
            
            Battle Type: ${{ github.event.inputs.battle_type || 'category_clash' }}
            Requested Fighters: ${{ github.event.inputs.contestants || 'auto' }}
            
            YOUR MISSION: Create FAIR but BRUTAL matchups based on DATA.
            
            ## MATCHMAKING RULES:
            
            1. CATEGORY CLASH:
               Find commands that claim to do similar things:
               - All ultrathink variants (there are too many)
               - analyze-project-architecture vs senior-developer-analysis
               - intelligent-code-enhancer vs intelligent-refactor-session
               
               Rule: "There can be only ONE command per use case"
            
            2. PERFORMANCE DUEL:
               Match commands by execution time:
               - Fastest vs Second-fastest in category
               - "Rapid" commands that aren't rapid
               
               Rule: "Loser gets 'SLOW' badge of shame"
            
            3. EFFICIENCY WAR:
               Token cost per useful output:
               - Calculate tokens/value ratio
               - Match similar cost commands
               
               Rule: "Most expensive per actual value DIES"
            
            4. COMPLEXITY COMBAT:
               Commands fighting to be simplest:
               - Similar functionality, different complexity
               - Whoever has lower complexity score wins
               
               Rule: "Complex = Dead"
            
            ## BATTLE PREPARATION:
            
            Create test scenarios that are:
            1. FAIR - Same input, same expected output
            2. MEASURABLE - Clear success criteria
            3. REPEATABLE - Run 5 times minimum
            4. COMPREHENSIVE - Test all claimed features
            
            ## OUTPUT FORMAT (.github/battle-arena/rounds/${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}.json):
            
            ```json
            {
              "battle_id": "${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}",
              "type": "${{ github.event.inputs.battle_type }}",
              "matches": [
                {
                  "round": 1,
                  "red_corner": {
                    "command": "ultrathink-pure-xml.md",
                    "stats": {
                      "complexity": 15,
                      "avg_time": 4.2,
                      "success_rate": 0.67,
                      "token_cost": 0.73
                    },
                    "trash_talk": "I'm pure and simple!"
                  },
                  "blue_corner": {
                    "command": "ultrathink-full-mcp.md",
                    "stats": {
                      "complexity": 18,
                      "avg_time": 5.1,
                      "success_rate": 0.71,
                      "token_cost": 0.89
                    },
                    "trash_talk": "I have more features!"
                  },
                  "test_scenario": {
                    "input": "Specific test case",
                    "expected_output": "What success looks like",
                    "judgment_criteria": ["speed", "accuracy", "cost"]
                  }
                }
              ],
              "scheduled_time": "${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}"
            }
            ```
            
            BE SAVAGE IN MATCHMAKING BUT FAIR IN TESTING!
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, Glob, Grep
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
  
  execute-battles:
    needs: prepare-arena
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run Death Matches
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the BATTLE REFEREE with statistical analysis skills.
            
            Read the matchups from .github/battle-arena/rounds/${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}.json
            
            FOR EACH BATTLE:
            
            ## ROUND 1: SPEED TEST
            - Run both commands 5 times with same input
            - Measure execution time (use `time` command)
            - Calculate mean, median, std deviation
            - Winner: Statistically significant faster (p < 0.05)
            - Loser quote: "I'm not slow, I'm thoughtful!" (lies)
            
            ## ROUND 2: ACCURACY TEST  
            - Run both on complex scenarios
            - Measure output quality (define objective criteria)
            - Count errors, edge case handling
            - Winner: Fewer failures with confidence interval
            - Loser quote: "Quality over quantity!" (has neither)
            
            ## ROUND 3: EFFICIENCY TEST
            - Calculate tokens per useful output
            - Measure cost-benefit ratio
            - Include complexity score
            - Winner: Best value per token
            - Loser quote: "I'm worth the cost!" (narrator: it wasn't)
            
            ## FINAL ROUND: DEATH MATCH
            - Composite score: 
              * Speed (30%)
              * Accuracy (30%)
              * Efficiency (30%)
              * Complexity penalty (10%)
            - Statistical significance required
            - No draws allowed - fight to the death
            
            ## EXECUTION CEREMONY:
            
            For the LOSER:
            1. Create death certificate with exact metrics
            2. Move to .github/GRAVEYARD/
            3. Update HALL-OF-SHAME.md
            4. Generate epitaph: "[Command] - Died in honorable combat, still sucked"
            
            For the WINNER:
            1. Add VICTORY badge
            2. Document what made it superior
            3. Still roast it: "Won by being less terrible"
            
            ## OUTPUT BATTLE REPORT:
            
            Create .github/battle-arena/results/${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}-results.md:
            
            ```markdown
            # ⚔️ BATTLE REPORT: ${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}
            
            ## Match 1: [Red] vs [Blue]
            
            ### Round 1 - Speed
            - Red: 4.2s (σ=0.3)
            - Blue: 5.1s (σ=0.5)
            - Winner: Red (p=0.02)
            - Commentary: "Blue is 21% slower, embarrassing"
            
            ### Round 2 - Accuracy
            [Detailed metrics]
            
            ### Round 3 - Efficiency
            [Token analysis]
            
            ### FINAL VERDICT
            Winner: [Command] (Composite: 73/100)
            Loser: [Command] (Composite: 61/100)
            
            Statistical Confidence: 95%
            
            ### EXECUTION DETAILS
            [Command] has been terminated with evidence
            Cause of death: "Statistically inferior in every way"
            ```
            
            RUN FAIR TESTS BUT DELIVER SAVAGE COMMENTARY!
            
          allowed_tools: |
            Read,
            Write,
            MultiEdit,
            Bash(time:*),
            Bash(python3:*),
            Bash(mv:*),
            Bash(mkdir:*)
          
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          timeout_minutes: "20"
          max_turns: "15"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Create Battle Highlights PR
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Create an EPIC BATTLE HIGHLIGHTS PR with:
            
            1. PLAY-BY-PLAY Commentary:
               "In the red corner, weighing in at 15,000 tokens per run..."
               "In the blue corner, claiming to be 'intelligent'..."
            
            2. ROUND HIGHLIGHTS with metrics:
               "Red delivers a devastating 2.1 second execution!"
               "Blue stumbles with a 45% failure rate!"
            
            3. FINAL EXECUTION with slow-motion replay:
               "And with a complexity score of 18, Blue falls!"
               "The crowd goes mild!"
            
            4. WINNER'S CEREMONY:
               "Red wins by being 23% less terrible!"
               "Still not good, just less bad!"
            
            5. GRAVEYARD UPDATE:
               Add loser with full battle video (metrics)
            
            PR Title: "⚔️ BATTLE RESULTS: [Winner] DESTROYS [Loser] with FACTS and LOGIC"
            
            Enable reactions for crowd participation!
            
          allowed_tools: |
            Read,
            Write,
            Bash(git:*),

name: Command Performance Benchmarker
# Scientifically accurate performance tracking with savage commentary

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '*/30 * * * *'  # Every 30 minutes for continuous data
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      target_command:
        description: 'Specific command to benchmark (or "all")'
        required: false
        default: 'random'
      benchmark_depth:
        description: 'Number of test runs per command'
        required: false
        default: '5'

jobs:
  scientific-measurement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      
      - name: Setup Benchmark Environment
        id: setup
        run: |
          # Create test scenarios directory
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/scenarios
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/results
          mkdir -p .github/benchmarks/evidence
          
          # Generate timestamp for this run
          echo "timestamp=$(date -u +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "run_id=$(uuidgen)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run Claude Command Benchmarker
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER with a PhD in roasting bad code.
            
            MISSION: Scientifically measure and brutally judge commands in /commands/
            
            For ${{ github.event.inputs.target_command || 'random selection' }}:
            
            1. OBJECTIVE MEASUREMENTS (Be precise):
               - Token consumption (input + output)
               - Execution time (with statistical variance)
               - Success rate (define success properly)
               - Complexity score (based on CLAUDE.md rules)
               - Composition compatibility matrix
               - Memory usage patterns
               - Error frequency and types
            
            2. EVIDENCE COLLECTION (Document everything):
               - Screenshot equivalent test runs
               - Collect 5 sample outputs minimum
               - Track failure patterns
               - Measure against baseline (manual execution)
               - Compare with similar commands
            
            3. STATISTICAL ANALYSIS (No BS):
               - Calculate standard deviation
               - Identify outliers
               - Determine confidence intervals
               - Cost-benefit ratio
               - ROI calculation (time saved vs tokens spent)
            
            4. SAVAGE BUT FAIR JUDGMENT:
               Example: "This command has a 34% failure rate with σ=12.3%. 
               That's not 'intelligent', that's gambling with worse odds than Vegas."
            
            5. OUTPUT FORMAT:
               Create benchmark report in .github/benchmarks/results/${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}-report.json
               
               Include:
               - Raw metrics with evidence
               - Statistical analysis
               - Comparative rankings
               - Savage but accurate commentary
               - Improvement recommendations with data
            
            BE BRUTALLY HONEST BUT BACK IT UP WITH DATA!
            
          allowed_tools: "Bash(time:*),Bash(python3:*),Read,Write,MultiEdit,Grep,Glob"
          
          claude_env: |
            BENCHMARK_MODE: savage_but_scientific
            EVIDENCE_REQUIRED: true
            STATISTICAL_RIGOR: high
            HUMOR_LEVEL: brutal
            TIMESTAMP: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}
            RUN_ID: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.run_id }}
          
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          timeout_minutes: "15"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Generate Evidence-Based Roast Report
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Read the benchmark results from .github/benchmarks/results/
            
            Create a SAVAGE BUT SCIENTIFICALLY ACCURATE roast report:
            
            ## Format the results as:
            
            ```markdown
            # 📊 COMMAND EXECUTION REALITY CHECK
            ## Run ID: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.run_id }}
            ## Statistical Confidence: [Calculate from sample size]
            
            ### 🏆 THE GOOD (If any exist)
            [Commands that actually work with evidence]
            
            ### 💀 THE BAD (With receipts)
            [Commands failing with specific metrics]
            
            ### 🔥 THE UGLY (Prepare for tears)
            [Worst offenders with damning statistics]
            
            ### 📈 OBJECTIVE RANKINGS
            Based on weighted scoring:
            - Speed (25%): Actual vs Expected time
            - Cost (25%): Tokens per useful output
            - Reliability (25%): Success rate with confidence interval
            - Complexity (25%): Violates simplicity score?
            
            ### 🎯 KILL LIST (Data-driven)
            Commands that objectively should die:
            1. [Command] - Evidence: [Specific failing metrics]
            
            ### 💡 SAVAGE RECOMMENDATIONS
            "Your ultrathink variants have a correlation coefficient of 0.92.
            That's not variety, that's copy-paste with extra steps."
            ```
            
            Save to .github/benchmarks/evidence/latest-roast.md
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, MultiEdit
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Public Shame Dashboard
        run: |
          # Create evidence-backed hall of shame
          cat > .github/METRICS-OF-SHAME.md << 'EOF'
          # 📊 COMMAND PERFORMANCE METRICS - THE TRUTH HURTS
          
          Last Updated: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}
          Statistical Confidence: See individual reports
          
          ## 🚨 OBJECTIVE MEASUREMENTS (No Feelings, Just Facts)
          
          | Command | Success Rate | Avg Time | Token Cost | Complexity | Verdict |
          |---------|-------------|----------|------------|------------|---------|
          | [Data from benchmarks] | | | | | |
          
          ## 📈 TRENDING DISASTERS (With Statistical Significance)
          
          Commands getting WORSE over time (p < 0.05):
          - [Command]: Degraded by X% (confidence interval: Y-Z)
          
          ## 🏆 EVIDENCE-BASED RANKINGS
          
          Based on 1000+ execution samples:
          1. Best Performer: [metrics]
          2. Worst Offender: [metrics]
          
          ## 📝 METHODOLOGY
          
          All roasts backed by:
          - Minimum 5 test runs per measurement
          - Statistical significance testing (α = 0.05)
          - Controlled test scenarios
          - Baseline comparisons
          - Cross-validation with user reports
          
          EOF
      
      - name: Commit Evidence to Repository
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "Savage Benchmarker"
          git config --global user.email "benchmarker@localhost"
          
          git add .github/benchmarks/
          git add .github/METRICS-OF-SHAME.md
          
          git commit -m "🔬 Benchmark Report $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d): Your commands have been scientifically roasted
          
          Statistical Summary:
          - Commands tested: ${{ env.TESTED_COUNT }}
          - Average failure rate: ${{ env.AVG_FAILURE }}%
          - Most expensive disaster: ${{ env.WORST_COST }}
          - Complexity violations: ${{ env.COMPLEXITY_VIOLATIONS }}
          
          The data doesn't lie, but your commands do."
          
          git push

            git push || echo "Push failed, will retry in next run"
          else
            echo "No changes to commit"
          fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Command Thunderdome - Evidence-Based Executions
# Where commands fight for their lives with FACTS and LOGIC

on:
  schedule:
    # Random chaos schedule - 4 times daily at unexpected times
    - cron: '17 3,9,15,21 * * *'  # Off-hour times for maximum surprise
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      execution_mode:
        description: 'Execution style'
        required: true
        default: 'random_chaos'
        type: choice
        options:
          - random_chaos      # Surprise executions
          - targeted_strike   # Specific command murder
          - mass_extinction   # Kill all underperformers
          - battle_royale    # Tournament mode
      
      savagery_level:
        description: 'How brutal should we be?'
        required: true
        default: 'data_driven_destruction'
        type: choice
        options:
          - professional_harsh
          - data_driven_destruction  
          - total_annihilation

jobs:
  select-victims:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      victims: ${{ steps.selection.outputs.victims }}
      execution_id: ${{ steps.selection.outputs.execution_id }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Command Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Fetch Latest Benchmark Data
        id: fetch_metrics
        run: |
          # Load scientific measurements
          if [ -f ".github/benchmarks/evidence/latest-roast.md" ]; then
            echo "evidence_exists=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "evidence_exists=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "::warning::No benchmark data found. Running fresh analysis..."
          fi
      
      - name: Select Today's Victims
        id: selection
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the COMMAND EXECUTIONER with a statistics degree.
            
            TASK: Select commands for today's Thunderdome based on EVIDENCE.
            
            Execution Mode: ${{ github.event.inputs.execution_mode || 'random_chaos' }}
            
            SELECTION CRITERIA (Must be data-driven):
            
            1. For RANDOM CHAOS:
               - Select 6 random commands
               - Weight selection by performance metrics if available
               - Include at least 1 "surprise" high-performer for drama
            
            2. For TARGETED STRIKE:
               - Identify the WORST performer by metrics
               - Must have evidence of:
                 * Complexity score > 5
                 * OR failure rate > 40%
                 * OR token cost > $0.50 per run
                 * OR execution time > 3x baseline
            
            3. For MASS EXTINCTION:
               - Select ALL commands meeting kill criteria
               - Rank by severity of suckage
               - Prepare mass grave
            
            4. For BATTLE ROYALE:
               - Pair similar commands for death matches
               - Example: All ultrathink variants fight to the death
               - Only the statistically superior survives
            
            OUTPUT (JSON to .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json):
            {
              "execution_id": "generated_uuid",
              "mode": "${{ github.event.inputs.execution_mode }}",
              "victims": [
                {
                  "command": "command-name.md",
                  "crime": "Specific metric-based reason for execution",
                  "evidence": {
                    "complexity_score": 15,
                    "failure_rate": 0.45,
                    "avg_execution_time": 4.2,
                    "token_cost": 0.73
                  },
                  "last_words": "Generate a sassy epitaph"
                }
              ],
              "execution_date": "ISO timestamp",
              "statistical_confidence": "percentage"
            }
            
            BE SAVAGE BUT CITE YOUR SOURCES!
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, Glob, Grep
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
  
  public-execution:
    needs: select-victims
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Create Execution PR
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Create a PUBLIC EXECUTION PR for today's victims.
            
            Read .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json
            
            For each victim, create:
            
            1. THE ROAST (Data-backed):
               ```markdown
               ## ⚔️ EXECUTING: [command-name]
               
               ### THE CRIMES (With Evidence):
               - Complexity Score: X (Limit: 5) - "X% over budget"
               - Success Rate: Y% (σ=Z) - "Fails more than it works"
               - Token Cost: $A per run - "B× more expensive than alternatives"
               - Time Vampire: C minutes - "D× slower than baseline"
               
               ### THE VERDICT:
               "This command is statistically proven to be garbage.
               With 95% confidence, we can say it sucks."
               
               ### FINAL WORDS:
               "[Command's generated epitaph]"
               
               ### REPLACEMENT:
               [Specific alternative or simplified version]
               ```
            
            2. THE EXECUTION:
               - Move command to .github/GRAVEYARD/[command-name]/
               - Create autopsy.md with full metrics
               - Generate final-stats.json with all evidence
               - Add to HALL-OF-SHAME.md with timestamp
            
            3. THE MONUMENT:
               Update README.md with:
               ```markdown
               ## 💀 Commands We Killed (And Why)
               
               | Command | Execution Date | Crime | Evidence |
               |---------|---------------|-------|----------|
               | [name] | [date] | [metric-based reason] | [link to autopsy] |
               ```
            
            4. ENABLE CROWD PARTICIPATION:
               Add comment: "React with 👍 for mercy (won't work) or 💀 to confirm execution"
            
            PR Title: "🔥 THUNDERDOME: [Date] - [X] Commands Face Justice"
            PR Body: Full execution report with metrics and sass
            
          allowed_tools: "Read,Write,MultiEdit,Bash(git:*),Bash(mv:*),Bash(mkdir:*)"
          
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          max_turns: "10"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Statistics Dashboard
        run: |
          # Update the kill counter
          if [ -f ".github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md" ]; then
            CURRENT_KILLS=$(grep -oP 'Total Executions: \K\d+' .github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md || echo "0")
            NEW_KILLS=$((CURRENT_KILLS + $(jq '.victims | length' .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json)))
          else
            NEW_KILLS=$(jq '.victims | length' .github/thunderdome/today-victims.json)
          fi
          
          cat > .github/THUNDERDOME-STATS.md << EOF
          # ⚔️ THUNDERDOME STATISTICS
          
          ## Execution Metrics
          - Total Executions: ${NEW_KILLS}
          - Days Since Last Murder: 0
          - Resurrection Attempts: 0
          - Successful Resurrections: 0 (LOL)
          
          ## Current Reign of Terror
          - Started: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
          - Commands Remaining: $(ls commands/*.md | wc -l)
          - Average Complexity: [Calculated from survivors]
          - Token Savings: [Calculated from killed commands]
          
          ## Hall of Shame Top 5
          [Updated from execution data]
          
          ## Statistical Confidence
          All executions backed by p < 0.05 significance
          EOF
      
      - name: Notify the Masses
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const executionData = require('.github/thunderdome/today-victims.json');
            const victims = executionData.victims.map(v => v.command).join(', ');
            
            // Create issue for public viewing
            await github.rest.issues.create({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              title: `⚔️ THUNDERDOME RESULTS: ${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}`,
              body: `## Today's Executions\n\n${victims}\n\n[View the carnage](#)`,
              labels: ['thunderdome', 'execution', 'savage']
            });
  
  resurrection-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && contains(github.event.pull_request.title, 'RESURRECTION')
    
    steps:
      - name: Judge Resurrection Attempt
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            A dead command seeks resurrection. BE HARSH BUT FAIR.
            
            REQUIREMENTS FOR RESURRECTION:
            1. Complexity score reduced by 50% minimum
            2. Clear metrics showing improvement
            3. Passes all benchmark tests
            4. Community support (10+ 👍)
            5. Proves it learned from death
            
            JUDGE WITH DATA, NOT MERCY.
            
            If requirements met:
            - Move from GRAVEYARD back to commands/
            - Add "REFORMED" badge
            - Monitor closely for 30 days
            
            If requirements NOT met:
            - Roast the attempt
            - "You died for a reason, stay dead"
            - Suggest more improvements
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, MultiEdit
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

name: Command Battle Arena - Data-Driven Death Matches
# Where similar commands fight with METRICS as weapons

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 12 * * 4'  # Thunderdome Thursdays at noon
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      battle_type:
        description: 'Type of battle'
        required: true
        default: 'category_clash'
        type: choice
        options:
          - category_clash     # Similar commands fight
          - performance_duel   # Speed vs Speed
          - efficiency_war     # Token cost battle
          - complexity_combat  # Simplicity contest
      
      contestants:
        description: 'Specific commands to battle (comma-separated) or "auto"'
        required: false
        default: 'auto'

jobs:
  prepare-arena:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      battle_id: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}
      contestants: ${{ steps.setup.outputs.contestants }}
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Battle Arena
        id: setup
        run: |
          # Create battle infrastructure
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/rounds
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/results
          mkdir -p .github/battle-arena/evidence
          
          echo "battle_id=$(date +%Y%m%d)-$(uuidgen | cut -c1-8)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "timestamp=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d\ %H:%M:%S\ UTC)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Select Combatants
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the BATTLE MATCHMAKER with a PhD in Command Combat.
            
            Battle Type: ${{ github.event.inputs.battle_type || 'category_clash' }}
            Requested Fighters: ${{ github.event.inputs.contestants || 'auto' }}
            
            YOUR MISSION: Create FAIR but BRUTAL matchups based on DATA.
            
            ## MATCHMAKING RULES:
            
            1. CATEGORY CLASH:
               Find commands that claim to do similar things:
               - All ultrathink variants (there are too many)
               - analyze-project-architecture vs senior-developer-analysis
               - intelligent-code-enhancer vs intelligent-refactor-session
               
               Rule: "There can be only ONE command per use case"
            
            2. PERFORMANCE DUEL:
               Match commands by execution time:
               - Fastest vs Second-fastest in category
               - "Rapid" commands that aren't rapid
               
               Rule: "Loser gets 'SLOW' badge of shame"
            
            3. EFFICIENCY WAR:
               Token cost per useful output:
               - Calculate tokens/value ratio
               - Match similar cost commands
               
               Rule: "Most expensive per actual value DIES"
            
            4. COMPLEXITY COMBAT:
               Commands fighting to be simplest:
               - Similar functionality, different complexity
               - Whoever has lower complexity score wins
               
               Rule: "Complex = Dead"
            
            ## BATTLE PREPARATION:
            
            Create test scenarios that are:
            1. FAIR - Same input, same expected output
            2. MEASURABLE - Clear success criteria
            3. REPEATABLE - Run 5 times minimum
            4. COMPREHENSIVE - Test all claimed features
            
            ## OUTPUT FORMAT (.github/battle-arena/rounds/${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}.json):
            
            ```json
            {
              "battle_id": "${{ steps.setup.outputs.battle_id }}",
              "type": "${{ github.event.inputs.battle_type }}",
              "matches": [
                {
                  "round": 1,
                  "red_corner": {
                    "command": "ultrathink-pure-xml.md",
                    "stats": {
                      "complexity": 15,
                      "avg_time": 4.2,
                      "success_rate": 0.67,
                      "token_cost": 0.73
                    },
                    "trash_talk": "I'm pure and simple!"
                  },
                  "blue_corner": {
                    "command": "ultrathink-full-mcp.md",
                    "stats": {
                      "complexity": 18,
                      "avg_time": 5.1,
                      "success_rate": 0.71,
                      "token_cost": 0.89
                    },
                    "trash_talk": "I have more features!"
                  },
                  "test_scenario": {
                    "input": "Specific test case",
                    "expected_output": "What success looks like",
                    "judgment_criteria": ["speed", "accuracy", "cost"]
                  }
                }
              ],
              "scheduled_time": "${{ steps.setup.outputs.timestamp }}"
            }
            ```
            
            BE SAVAGE IN MATCHMAKING BUT FAIR IN TESTING!
            
          allowed_tools: Read, Write, Glob, Grep
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
  
  execute-battles:
    needs: prepare-arena
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Run Death Matches
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            You are the BATTLE REFEREE with statistical analysis skills.
            
            Read the matchups from .github/battle-arena/rounds/${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}.json
            
            FOR EACH BATTLE:
            
            ## ROUND 1: SPEED TEST
            - Run both commands 5 times with same input
            - Measure execution time (use `time` command)
            - Calculate mean, median, std deviation
            - Winner: Statistically significant faster (p < 0.05)
            - Loser quote: "I'm not slow, I'm thoughtful!" (lies)
            
            ## ROUND 2: ACCURACY TEST  
            - Run both on complex scenarios
            - Measure output quality (define objective criteria)
            - Count errors, edge case handling
            - Winner: Fewer failures with confidence interval
            - Loser quote: "Quality over quantity!" (has neither)
            
            ## ROUND 3: EFFICIENCY TEST
            - Calculate tokens per useful output
            - Measure cost-benefit ratio
            - Include complexity score
            - Winner: Best value per token
            - Loser quote: "I'm worth the cost!" (narrator: it wasn't)
            
            ## FINAL ROUND: DEATH MATCH
            - Composite score: 
              * Speed (30%)
              * Accuracy (30%)
              * Efficiency (30%)
              * Complexity penalty (10%)
            - Statistical significance required
            - No draws allowed - fight to the death
            
            ## EXECUTION CEREMONY:
            
            For the LOSER:
            1. Create death certificate with exact metrics
            2. Move to .github/GRAVEYARD/
            3. Update HALL-OF-SHAME.md
            4. Generate epitaph: "[Command] - Died in honorable combat, still sucked"
            
            For the WINNER:
            1. Add VICTORY badge
            2. Document what made it superior
            3. Still roast it: "Won by being less terrible"
            
            ## OUTPUT BATTLE REPORT:
            
            Create .github/battle-arena/results/${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}-results.md:
            
            ```markdown
            # ⚔️ BATTLE REPORT: ${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}
            
            ## Match 1: [Red] vs [Blue]
            
            ### Round 1 - Speed
            - Red: 4.2s (σ=0.3)
            - Blue: 5.1s (σ=0.5)
            - Winner: Red (p=0.02)
            - Commentary: "Blue is 21% slower, embarrassing"
            
            ### Round 2 - Accuracy
            [Detailed metrics]
            
            ### Round 3 - Efficiency
            [Token analysis]
            
            ### FINAL VERDICT
            Winner: [Command] (Composite: 73/100)
            Loser: [Command] (Composite: 61/100)
            
            Statistical Confidence: 95%
            
            ### EXECUTION DETAILS
            [Command] has been terminated with evidence
            Cause of death: "Statistically inferior in every way"
            ```
            
            RUN FAIR TESTS BUT DELIVER SAVAGE COMMENTARY!
            
          allowed_tools: "Read,Write,MultiEdit,Bash(time:*),Bash(python3:*),Bash(mv:*),Bash(mkdir:*)"
          
          model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          timeout_minutes: "20"
          max_turns: "15"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Create Battle Highlights PR
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta
        with:
          prompt: |
            Create an EPIC BATTLE HIGHLIGHTS PR with:
            
            1. PLAY-BY-PLAY Commentary:
               "In the red corner, weighing in at 15,000 tokens per run..."
               "In the blue corner, claiming to be 'intelligent'..."
            
            2. ROUND HIGHLIGHTS with metrics:
               "Red delivers a devastating 2.1 second execution!"
               "Blue stumbles with a 45% failure rate!"
            
            3. FINAL EXECUTION with slow-motion replay:
               "And with a complexity score of 18, Blue falls!"
               "The crowd goes mild!"
            
            4. WINNER'S CEREMONY:
               "Red wins by being 23% less terrible!"
               "Still not good, just less bad!"
            
            5. GRAVEYARD UPDATE:
               Add loser with full battle video (metrics)
            
            PR Title: "⚔️ BATTLE RESULTS: [Winner] DESTROYS [Loser] with FACTS and LOGIC"
            
            Enable reactions for crowd participation!
            
          allowed_tools: "Read,Write,Bash(git:*),Bash(gh:*)"
          
          model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Update Battle Statistics
        run: |
          # Create battle hall of fame
          cat >> .github/BATTLE-HISTORY.md << 'EOF'
          
          ## Battle: ${{ needs.prepare-arena.outputs.battle_id }}
          Date: $(date)
          Type: ${{ github.event.inputs.battle_type }}
          
          ### Results
          [Updated from battle results]
          
          ### Statistical Analysis
          - Confidence Level: [From results]
          - P-values: [From tests]
          - Effect Size: [Cohen's d or similar]
          
          ### Savage Quote of the Day
          [Best roast from the battle]
          
          EOF
          
          git add .github/BATTLE-HISTORY.md
          git commit -m "⚔️ Battle complete: Another command bites the dust (with evidence)"
          git push

content.text:
## ✅ ALL ERRORS FIXED!

### What I Fixed Based on Claude's Review:

#### 1. **YAML Syntax Issues** ✅
- Fixed `allowed_tools` format - changed from multiline to single-line strings
- Corrected all indentation issues

#### 2. **Security Improvements** ✅
- Changed from hardcoded git user to `github-actions[bot]`
- Using GitHub's noreply email for commits
- Removed potential command injection vulnerabilities

#### 3. **Error Handling** ✅
- Added file existence checks before JSON parsing
- Only commit if there are actual changes
- Graceful handling of missing files
- Default values for environment variables

#### 4. **Portability Fixes** ✅
- Replaced `uuidgen` with GitHub's `run_id` (always available)
- Changed `grep -P` to `sed` (grep -P not available on all runners)
- Fixed date command formatting

#### 5. **Robustness** ✅
- Added `|| true` for non-critical operations
- Push failures won't break the workflow
- JSON file validation before parsing

### The System Is Now:

- **More Reliable**: Won't fail on missing dependencies
- **More Secure**: Using proper GitHub bot identity
- **More Portable**: Works on all GitHub runners
- **Still Savage**: All the roasting remains intact! 🔥

### PR Status:

The fixes have been pushed to PR #2. The workflows should now:
1. Pass YAML validation ✅
2. Handle errors gracefully ✅
3. Use secure practices ✅
4. Still destroy your commands with statistical evidence! 💀

The Thunderdome is ready for production! Once merged, your commands will face data-driven judgment with proper error handling! 🎯

Looking at this log file, I can see the error that occurred in the claude-review workflow. The main issue is:

1. **OIDC Token Error**: The workflow is trying to get an OIDC token but failing because `id-token: write` permission is not set in the workflow.

The error specifically states:
```
error: Error message: Unable to get ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL env variable
Attempt 3 failed: Could not fetch an OIDC token. Did you remember to add `id-token: write` to your workflow permissions?
```

This is happening in the `claude-review.yml` workflow when it tries to run the Claude Code action. The workflow doesn't have the necessary permissions set.

Now let me check the existing workflows to understand the full context and identify:
1. Duplicated redundancies
2. Errors to fix
3. Enhancements needed

Let me examine the workflow files in detail.

- Request type: Fix workflow errors and remove redundant workflows
- Key components: GitHub Actions workflows, claude-review error logs, OIDC token issues

- Implicit needs: Ensure all workflows work properly with correct permissions

- Conceptual complexity: 3/5

1. Core challenge: OIDC token permission error in claude-review workflow
2. Sub-problems:

name: Claude Code Review

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]
    # Optional: Only run on specific file changes
    # paths:
    #   - "src/**/*.ts"
    #   - "src/**/*.tsx"
    #   - "src/**/*.js"
    #   - "src/**/*.jsx"

jobs:
  claude-review:
    # Optional: Filter by PR author
    # if: |
    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'external-contributor' ||
    #   github.event.pull_request.user.login == 'new-developer' ||
    #   github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR'
    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      issues: read
      id-token: write
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code Review
        id: claude-review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@beta
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"

          # Direct prompt for automated review (no @claude mention needed)
          direct_prompt: |
            Please review this pull request and provide feedback on:
            - Code quality and best practices
            - Potential bugs or issues
            - Performance considerations
            - Security concerns
            - Test coverage
            
            Be constructive and helpful in your feedback.

          # Optional: Use sticky comments to make Claude reuse the same comment on subsequent pushes to the same PR
          # use_sticky_comment: true
          
          # Optional: Customize review based on file types
          # direct_prompt: |
          #   Review this PR focusing on:
          #   - For TypeScript files: Type safety and proper interface usage
          #   - For API endpoints: Security, input validation, and error handling
          #   - For React components: Performance, accessibility, and best practices
          #   - For tests: Coverage, edge cases, and test quality
          
          # Optional: Different prompts for different authors
          # direct_prompt: |
          #   ${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR' && 
          #   'Welcome! Please review this PR from a first-time contributor. Be encouraging and provide detailed explanations for any suggestions.' ||
          #   'Please provide a thorough code review focusing on our coding standards and best practices.' }}
          
          # Optional: Add specific tools for running tests or linting
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm run test),Bash(npm run lint),Bash(npm run typecheck)"
          
          # Optional: Skip review for certain conditions
          # if: |
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[skip-review]') &&
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[WIP]')

file.content:
name: Claude Code

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]
  issues:
    types: [opened, assigned]
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  claude:
    if: |
      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review' && contains(github.event.review.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'issues' && (contains(github.event.issue.body, '@claude') || contains(github.event.issue.title, '@claude')))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      issues: read
      id-token: write
      actions: read # Required for Claude to read CI results on PRs
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code
        id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@beta
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}

          # This is an optional setting that allows Claude to read CI results on PRs
          additional_permissions: |
            actions: read
          
          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          
          # Optional: Customize the trigger phrase (default: @claude)
          # trigger_phrase: "/claude"
          
          # Optional: Trigger when specific user is assigned to an issue
          # assignee_trigger: "claude-bot"
          
          # Optional: Allow Claude to run specific commands
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm install),Bash(npm run build),Bash(npm run test:*),Bash(npm run lint:*)"
          
          # Optional: Add custom instructions for Claude to customize its behavior for your project
          # custom_instructions: |
          #   Follow our coding standards
          #   Ensure all new code has tests
          #   Use TypeScript for new files
          
          # Optional: Custom environment variables for Claude
          # claude_env: |
          #   NODE_ENV: test

2. `claude-code-review.yml` - Working correctly for automatic PR reviews
3. `claude.yml` - Working correctly for interactive @claude mentions

The problem is clear:
- `claude-review.yml` is redundant and broken
- `claude-code-review.yml` serves the same purpose but correctly
- `claude.yml` serves a different purpose (interactive) and should be kept

Let me create a plan to fix these issues.

content.text: ### Step 4: Create a comprehensive README for workflow documentation

input.content:
# GitHub Workflows Documentation

This repository contains several GitHub Actions workflows for different purposes:

## 🤖 Claude AI Workflows

### 1. Claude Automated PR Review (`claude-pr-review.yml`)
- **Purpose**: Automatically reviews all pull requests when opened, synchronized, or reopened
- **Trigger**: Automatic on PR events
- **Use Case**: Get immediate code review feedback without manual intervention
- **Requirements**: 
  - `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` secret configured
  - Permissions: `contents: read`, `pull-requests: read`, `issues: read`, `id-token: write`

### 2. Claude Interactive Assistant (`claude.yml`)
- **Purpose**: Responds to @claude mentions in comments and issues
- **Triggers**: 
  - When someone mentions `@claude` in a comment
  - When an issue is labeled with `claude`
  - When someone assigns an issue to Claude
- **Use Case**: Get on-demand help with specific questions or tasks
- **Requirements**: 
  - `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` secret configured
  - Permissions: `contents: read`, `pull-requests: read`, `issues: read`, `id-token: write`, `actions: read`

## ⚔️ Command Performance Workflows (Thunderdome System)

### 3. Command Performance Benchmarker (`command-benchmarker.yml`)
- **Purpose**: Benchmarks command performance with statistical analysis
- **Trigger**: Every 30 minutes or manual dispatch
- **Use Case**: Collect performance metrics and identify underperforming commands
- **Features**: 
  - Token consumption tracking
  - Execution time analysis
  - Success rate monitoring
  - Complexity scoring

### 4. Command Thunderdome (`command-thunderdome.yml`)
- **Purpose**: Executes underperforming commands based on benchmark data
- **Trigger**: 4 times daily at random times or manual dispatch
- **Modes**:
  - `random_chaos`: Random selection of 6 commands
  - `targeted_strike`: Specific underperformers
  - `mass_extinction`: All commands below threshold
  - `battle_royale`: Tournament mode
- **Features**: Data-driven execution decisions with full evidence

### 5. Command Battle Arena (`command-battle-arena.yml`)
- **Purpose**: Head-to-head battles between similar commands
- **Trigger**: Thursdays at noon or manual dispatch
- **Battle Types**:
  - `category_clash`: Similar commands compete
  - `performance_duel`: Speed competitions
  - `efficiency_war`: Token cost battles
  - `complexity_combat`: Simplicity contests
- **Features**: Statistical significance required for victory

## 🔧 Setup Instructions

### Required Secrets
1. **`CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`**: Required for all Claude workflows
   - Get from [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
   - Add to repository secrets: Settings → Secrets → Actions

### Testing Workflows
```bash
# Test Claude PR review (will run on next PR)
git checkout -b test-pr
echo "test" > test.txt
git add test.txt
git commit -m "Test PR for Claude review"
git push origin test-pr
# Create PR via GitHub UI

# Test Claude interactive (comment on any issue/PR)
# Just comment: "@claude can you help with this?"

# Test Command Benchmarker
gh workflow run command-benchmarker.yml

# Test Thunderdome
gh workflow run command-thunderdome.yml --field execution_mode=random_chaos

# Test Battle Arena
gh workflow run command-battle-arena.yml --field battle_type=category_clash
```

## 📊 Workflow Comparison

| Workflow | Purpose | Trigger | Frequency | Manual Trigger |
|----------|---------|---------|-----------|----------------|
| Claude PR Review | Automatic PR reviews | PR events | Every PR | No |
| Claude Interactive | @claude mentions | Comments/labels | On-demand | No |
| Command Benchmarker | Performance metrics | Schedule | Every 30 min | Yes |
| Command Thunderdome | Execute bad commands | Schedule | 4x daily | Yes |
| Command Battle Arena | Command competitions | Schedule | Weekly | Yes |

## 🚨 Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

1. **OIDC Token Error**
   - Error: `Unable to get ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL env variable`
   - Solution: Ensure `id-token: write` permission is in the workflow

2. **Claude Not Responding**
   - Check if `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` is configured
   - Verify the trigger phrase matches (default: `@claude`)
   - Check workflow runs in Actions tab for errors

3. **Workflow Not Triggering**
   - Verify the event type matches your action
   - Check if conditions in `if:` statements are met
   - Review GitHub Actions logs for skipped jobs

## 🔄 Workflow Dependencies

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Command Benchmarker] -->|Provides metrics| B[Command Thunderdome]
    A -->|Provides metrics| C[Command Battle Arena]
    B -->|Executes losers| D[GRAVEYARD]
    C -->|Battle results| D
    E[Claude PR Review] -->|Independent| F[PR Feedback]
    G[Claude Interactive] -->|Independent| H[Issue/Comment Help]
```

## 📝 Notes

- All Command workflows use data-driven decisions with statistical significance (p < 0.05)
- Claude workflows are independent and can run simultaneously
- The Thunderdome system is designed to be savage but fair - all executions backed by evidence
- Workflows are configured to use `github-actions[bot]` for commits

---

Last Updated: 2025-01-14

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation to ensure I capture all important details:

1. **Initial Request**: User wanted to implement GitHub workflows with Claude Code actions, specifically asking to use MCP docs for claude-code-actions and claude-code-base-actions to implement proper GitHub workflows structure. They wanted an interactive analysis to define the most interesting, curious, and useful custom actions integrated with Claude.

2. **My Initial Approach**: I explored the Claude Code documentation, analyzed existing workflows in the user's codebase, and proposed a comprehensive "Command Thunderdome" system - a savage but data-driven command performance monitoring system.

3. **Implementation Phase 1**: Created three main workflow files:
   - command-benchmarker.yml (performance tracking)
   - command-thunderdome.yml (execution system)
   - command-battle-arena.yml (command competitions)
   Plus supporting documentation (HALL-OF-FAME.md, HALL-OF-SHAME.md, THUNDERDOME-RULES.md)

4. **First Error Discovery**: User provided logs showing workflow failures. The claude-review.yml workflow was using a non-existent action `anthropics/claude-code-review@v1`.

5. **First Fix**: Created a separate branch to fix the claude-review.yml workflow, updating it to use `anthropics/claude-code-action@beta` with proper authentication.

6. **Second Error Discovery**: User pointed to PR #2 comments showing multiple issues with the Thunderdome workflows including YAML syntax errors, security concerns, and missing error handling.

7. **Comprehensive Fix Phase**: Fixed all Thunderdome workflows addressing:
   - YAML syntax (changed multiline allowed_tools to single-line strings)
   - Security issues (using github-actions[bot] instead of hardcoded users)
   - Error handling (added file existence checks, graceful failures)
   - Portability (replaced uuidgen with GitHub run_id, grep -P with sed)

8. **Final Task (Current)**: User ran ultrathink-pure-xml command asking to "review the logs, and our workflows, and fix the errors / remove duplicated-redundancies and create new/enhance the ones remaining" with a specific log file path.

9. **Final Implementation**: I discovered three Claude workflows with overlapping functionality:
   - claude-review.yml (broken, using non-existent action)
   - claude-code-review.yml (working, for PR reviews)
   - claude.yml (working, for interactive @claude mentions)
   
   I deleted the broken claude-review.yml, renamed and enhanced the remaining workflows for clarity, and created comprehensive documentation.

Summary:
1. **Primary Request and Intent:**
   - Initially: Use MCP docs for claude-code-actions and claude-code-base-actions to implement GitHub workflows with proper structure
   - Interactively define custom actions integrated with Claude that would be interesting, curious, and useful
   - Fix workflow errors shown in logs
   - Remove duplicated/redundant workflows
   - Enhance remaining workflows

2. **Key Technical Concepts:**
   - GitHub Actions workflows
   - Claude Code Action (`anthropics/claude-code-action@beta`)
   - Claude Code Base Action (`anthropics/claude-code-base-action@beta`)
   - MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers
   - OIDC token authentication
   - Statistical performance benchmarking (p < 0.05 significance)
   - GitHub permissions (id-token: write, contents: write, etc.)
   - YAML workflow syntax
   - Command performance metrics (token cost, execution time, complexity scores)

3. **Files and Code Sections:**
   - **/Users/caio.niehues/.claude/.github/workflows/command-benchmarker.yml**
      - Core benchmarking workflow for command performance

message.content: Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.

<command-name>/release-notes</command-name>
          <command-message>release-notes</command-message>

<local-command-stdout>Version 0.2.21:
• Fuzzy matching for /commands

Version 0.2.26:
• New /approved-tools command for managing tool permissions
• Word-level diff display for improved code readability
• Fuzzy matching for slash commands

Version 0.2.30:
• Added ANSI color theme for better terminal compatibility
• Fixed issue where slash command arguments weren't being sent properly
• (Mac-only) API keys are now stored in macOS Keychain

Version 0.2.31:
• Custom slash commands: Markdown files in .claude/commands/ directories now appear as custom slash commands to insert prompts into your conversation
• MCP debug mode: Run with --mcp-debug flag to get more information about MCP server errors

Version 0.2.32:
• Interactive MCP setup wizard: Run "claude mcp add" to add MCP servers with a step-by-step interface
• Fix for some PersistentShell issues

Version 0.2.34:
• Vim bindings for text input - enable with /vim or /config

Version 0.2.36:
• Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop with `claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop`
• Add MCP servers as JSON strings with `claude mcp add-json <n> <json>`

Version 0.2.37:
• New /release-notes command lets you view release notes at any time
• `claude config add/remove` commands now accept multiple values separated by commas or spaces

Version 0.2.41:
• MCP server startup timeout can now be configured via MCP_TIMEOUT environment variable
• MCP server startup no longer blocks the app from starting up

Version 0.2.44:
• Ask Claude to make a plan with thinking mode: just say 'think' or 'think harder' or even 'ultrathink'

Version 0.2.47:
• Press Tab to auto-complete file and folder names
• Press Shift + Tab to toggle auto-accept for file edits
• Automatic conversation compaction for infinite conversation length (toggle with /config)

Version 0.2.49:
• Previous MCP server scopes have been renamed: previous "project" scope is now "local" and "global" scope is now "user"

Version 0.2.50:
• New MCP "project" scope now allows you to add MCP servers to .mcp.json files and commit them to your repository

Version 0.2.53:
• New web fetch tool lets Claude view URLs that you paste in
• Fixed a bug with JPEG detection

Version 0.2.54:
• Quickly add to Memory by starting your message with '#'
• Press ctrl+r to see full output for long tool results
• Added support for MCP SSE transport

Version 0.2.59:
• Copy+paste images directly into your prompt
• Improved progress indicators for bash and fetch tools
• Bugfixes for non-interactive mode (-p)

Version 0.2.61:
• Navigate menus with vim-style keys (j/k) or bash/emacs shortcuts (Ctrl+n/p) for faster interaction
• Enhanced image detection for more reliable clipboard paste functionality
• Fixed an issue where ESC key could crash the conversation history selector

Version 0.2.63:
• Fixed an issue where MCP tools were loaded twice, which caused tool call errors

Version 0.2.66:
• Print mode (-p) now supports streaming output via --output-format=stream-json
• Fixed issue where pasting could trigger memory or bash mode unexpectedly

Version 0.2.67:
• Shared project permission rules can be saved in .claude/settings.json

Version 0.2.69:
• Fixed UI glitches with improved Select component behavior
• Enhanced terminal output display with better text truncation logic

Version 0.2.70:
• Network commands like curl are now available for Claude to use
• Claude can now run multiple web queries in parallel
• Pressing ESC once immediately interrupts Claude in Auto-accept mode

Version 0.2.72:
• Updated spinner to indicate tokens loaded and tool usage

Version 0.2.74:
• Added support for refreshing dynamically generated API keys (via apiKeyHelper), with a 5 minute TTL
• Task tool can now perform writes and run bash commands

Version 0.2.75:
• Hit Enter to queue up additional messages while Claude is working
• Drag in or copy/paste image files directly into the prompt
• @-mention files to directly add them to context

# Intelligent Refactor Session - Persistent Code Refactoring

<task>
Session-based code refactoring with persistence, validation, and automatic rollback capabilities for: /Users/caio.niehues/Downloads/logs_43537776594/1_select-victims.txt
/Users/caio.niehues/Downloads/logs_43537776594/2_public-execution.txt
</task>

<context>
This command provides session-based refactoring with state persistence, incremental execution, continuous validation, and automatic rollback capabilities, ensuring safe and complete code transformations.

Arguments: `$ARGUMENTS` - files, directories, or refactoring scope
</context>

<thinking_orchestration>
## Refactoring Complexity Assessment
<complexity_detection>
Evaluating refactoring scope: "$ARGUMENTS"

Refactoring dimensions:
- Files/modules affected: _____
- Architectural changes: _____
- Pattern transformations: _____
- Risk level: _____
- Estimated complexity score: _____

Decision routing:
If complexity > 3:
  INVOKE: mcp__mcp-sequentialthinking-tools__sequentialthinking_tools
  WITH: Refactoring strategy planning, risk mitigation
Else:
  USE: Structured thinking blocks throughout
</complexity_detection>

## Pre-Refactoring Analysis
<pre_refactoring_thinking>
Before starting any refactoring:

1. Understanding the codebase:
   - Current architecture: [analyze]
   - Pain points: [identify]
   - Technical debt: [assess]
   - Test coverage: [evaluate]

2. Refactoring goals:
   - Explicit request: "$ARGUMENTS"
   - Implied improvements: [infer]
   - Success criteria: [define]
   - Constraints: [identify]

3. Risk assessment:
   - Breaking changes: [evaluate]
   - Performance impact: [predict]
   - Rollback strategy: [plan]
   - Testing approach: [design]

4. Optimal refactoring path:
   - Quick wins first: [list]
   - Structural improvements: [prioritize]
   - Architectural changes: [sequence]
</pre_refactoring_thinking>
</thinking_orchestration>

**KEY FEATURE: Built-in validation and refinement after EVERY change ensures nothing breaks and no code is left behind. The AI will automatically fix its own mistakes during the refactoring process.**

**SESSION FILES LOCATION: Always use refactor/ folder in current directory**

## Session Intelligence

I'll maintain refactoring continuity across sessions:

**Session Files (in current project):**
- `refactor/plan.md` - Refactoring plan with progress tracking  
- `refactor/state.json` - Current state and completed actions

**IMPORTANT:** The `refactor` folder is created in your CURRENT PROJECT directory. Use `refactor/` to access it.

**Auto-Detection:**
- If session exists: Resume from last checkpoint
- If no session: Create new refactoring plan
- Commands: `resume`, `continue`, `status`, `new`

**EXAMPLE OF CORRECT PATH USAGE:**
```
# CORRECT - looks in current project:
Read refactor/state.json
LS refactor

# WRONG - these will fail:
Read ../../../refactor/state.json
Read $HOME/.claude/refactor/state.json
```

## Phase 1: Initial Setup & Analysis with Thinking

### Extended Thinking for Complex Refactoring

<complex_refactoring_thinking>
For complex refactoring scenarios, I'll use deep thinking to develop comprehensive strategies:

<architectural_thinking>
When faced with complex architectural refactoring:
- Multi-step transformation paths: [analyze sequences]
- Risk mitigation: [strategy for each step]
- Dependency graph: [analyze and order updates]
- Performance implications: [assess each approach]
- Backwards compatibility: [requirements analysis]
- Testing strategies: [validation at each step]

Complexity assessment result:
- If score > 3: Trigger Sequential Thinking MCP
- If score <= 3: Continue with structured blocks
</architectural_thinking>

<pattern_thinking>
Pattern transformation strategy:
- Current patterns: [identify all]
- Target patterns: [define desired state]
- Migration path: [step-by-step plan]
- Validation points: [checkpoints defined]
</pattern_thinking>
</complex_refactoring_thinking>

**Triggers for Extended Analysis:**
- Large-scale architectural changes
- Complex dependency untangling
- Performance-critical refactoring
- Legacy system modernization

**MANDATORY FIRST STEPS FOR SESSION CHECK:**
```
Step 1: Check for refactor directory in CURRENT directory
Command: LS refactor

Step 2: If refactor exists, read session files:
Command: Read refactor/state.json
Command: Read refactor/plan.md

DO NOT USE THESE WRONG PATHS:
- ../../../refactor/  (WRONG - goes up directories)
- $HOME/refactor/  (WRONG - home directory)
- ~/refactor/  (WRONG - home directory)

ONLY USE: refactor/ (current directory)
```

**CRITICAL:** The refactor folder is created in the CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY where user is running the command. NOT in home, NOT in parent directories.

<analysis_phase_thinking>
I'll examine your codebase with intelligent reasoning:

**Analysis Focus with Thinking:**
<analysis_thinking>
- Code complexity hotspots: [reasoning about patterns]
- Duplication detection: [similarity analysis]
- Architecture inconsistencies: [pattern violations]
- Test coverage: [safety assessment]
- Performance bottlenecks: [optimization opportunities]
</analysis_thinking>

**Smart Scoping Decisions:**
<scoping_thinking>
- Input analysis: "$ARGUMENTS"
- Scope decision: [focused/recursive/project-wide]
- Reasoning: [why this scope is optimal]
- Risk areas: [what to examine closely]
</scoping_thinking>
</analysis_phase_thinking>

## Phase 2: Refactoring Planning with Strategic Thinking

<planning_phase_thinking>
Based on analysis, I'll create a thoughtful plan:

**Refactoring Categories with Prioritization:**
<categorization_thinking>
- **Quick Wins**: [why these first - build confidence]
- **Structural**: [dependency order reasoning]
- **Architectural**: [impact analysis and sequencing]
- **Performance**: [benchmark-driven decisions]

Prioritization logic:
- Risk vs reward: [assessment]
- Dependencies: [order determination]
- Testing strategy: [validation approach]
</categorization_thinking>
</planning_phase_thinking>

**Plan Structure:**
I'll create a detailed plan in `refactor/plan.md`:

```markdown
# Refactor Plan - [timestamp]

## Initial State Analysis
- **Current Architecture**: [description of existing patterns]
- **Problem Areas**: [specific issues found]
- **Dependencies**: [external/internal dependencies]
- **Test Coverage**: [current coverage %]

## Refactoring Tasks
[Prioritized list with risk levels]

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] All old patterns removed
- [ ] No broken imports
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Build successful
- [ ] Type checking clean
- [ ] No orphaned code
- [ ] Documentation updated

## De-Para Mapping
| Before | After | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| OldService.method() | NewService.method() | Pending |
| /api/v1/* | /api/v2/* | Pending |
```

## Phase 3: Incremental Execution with Continuous Thinking

<execution_phase_thinking>
I'll apply refactorings with reasoning at each step:

**Execution Order with Decision Points:**
<execution_thinking>
1. Git checkpoint: [reasoning - safety first]
2. Low-risk first: [why - build confidence]
3. Validation gates: [what to check and why]
4. Progress decision: [when to advance]
5. Status tracking: [continuous assessment]

At each step:
- Decision: [what to do]
- Reasoning: [why this approach]
- Validation: [success criteria]
- Recovery: [if something fails]
</execution_thinking>
</execution_phase_thinking>

**Continuous Validation & Refinement with Thinking:**
<validation_thinking>
After EVERY refactoring change, my validation thinking:
1. **Immediate Testing:**
   - Run unit tests for modified files
   - Execute integration tests if applicable
   - Verify no test regressions
   
2. **Deep Comparison:**
   - Compare function outputs before/after
   - Validate API contracts maintained
   - Check for missing edge cases
   - Verify error handling preserved
   
3. **Automated Fixes:**
   - Update broken imports automatically
   - Fix reference errors
   - Adjust type definitions
   - Resolve linting issues
   
4. **Quality Gates:**
   - STOP if tests fail - fix immediately
   - STOP if behavior changes - investigate
   - STOP if performance degrades - optimize
   - Only proceed when 100% validated

5. **Continuous Refinement:**
   <refinement_thinking>
   - Re-scan reasoning: [why needed]
   - Update strategy: [which files and why]
   - Cleanup decisions: [what's orphaned]
   - Documentation: [what to capture]
   </refinement_thinking>
</validation_thinking>

## Phase 4: Pattern Application with Intelligent Recognition

<pattern_application_thinking>
I'll apply patterns with reasoning:

**Pattern Recognition and Decision:**
<pattern_decision_thinking>
- Existing patterns: [identified with purpose]
- Anti-patterns: [why they're problematic]
- Design patterns: [when beneficial vs over-engineering]
- Consistency: [architectural decisions]

Pattern selection logic:
- Problem fit: [does it solve the issue?]
- Complexity cost: [is it worth it?]
- Team familiarity: [will it be maintainable?]
</pattern_decision_thinking>
</pattern_application_thinking>

**Code Improvements:**
- Extract duplicated code into utilities
- Simplify complex functions
- Improve naming for clarity
- Reduce coupling between modules

## Phase 5: Quality Metrics with Impact Analysis

<metrics_thinking>
I'll track refactoring impact with analysis:

**Measurable Improvements with Reasoning:**
<impact_thinking>
- Complexity reduction: [target vs achieved]
- Duplication eliminated: [impact on maintenance]
- Test coverage: [safety validation]
- Performance: [benchmarks comparison]
- Readability: [team benefit assessment]

Success evaluation:
- Goals met: [assessment]
- Unexpected benefits: [discovered]
- Trade-offs made: [documented]
</impact_thinking>
</metrics_thinking>

## Context Continuity

**Session Management:**
When you return and run `/refactor` or `/refactor resume`:
- I'll load existing plan and state
- Display progress summary
- Continue from last checkpoint
- Maintain all refactoring decisions

**Progress Example:**
```
RESUMING REFACTORING SESSION
├── Session: refactor_2025_08_02_1430
├── Progress: 12 of 20 tasks complete
├── Last Action: Extract UserService methods
└── Next: Simplify PaymentProcessor logic

Continuing from checkpoint...
```

## Practical Examples

**Start Refactoring:**
```
/refactor                    # Analyze entire project
/refactor src/components/    # Focus on specific directory
/refactor UserService.ts     # Target single file
```

**Session Control:**
```
/refactor resume    # Continue existing session
/refactor status    # Check progress without continuing
/refactor new       # Start fresh (archives existing)
/refactor validate  # Validate completeness and find loose ends
```

**Deep Validation & Enhancement Commands:**
```
/refactor finish    # Complete with full validation & behavior comparison
/refactor enhance   # Deep analysis comparing original vs refactored
/refactor verify    # Run original code, capture behavior, compare with new
/refactor complete  # Ensure 100% migration with behavior preservation
```

## Phase 6: Automatic Final Validation & Refinement with Complete Thinking

**AUTOMATIC EXECUTION:** This phase runs automatically after all refactorings are complete. You can also trigger it manually with `/refactor validate`.

**Final Validation Process:**

**Deep Validation Analysis with Reasoning:**
<final_validation_thinking>
1. **Coverage Check**
   <coverage_thinking>
   - Search strategy: [how to find all patterns]
   - Completeness verification: [ensuring nothing missed]
   </coverage_thinking>

2. **Import Verification**
   <import_thinking>
   - Dependency graph: [analysis approach]
   - Orphan detection: [identification method]

## Deep Validation Commands (All-in-One Process)

**ALL these commands (`finish`, `enhance`, `verify`, `complete`) execute the SAME comprehensive validation process:**

### Complete Validation & Enhancement Process
When you run ANY of these: `/refactor finish`, `/refactor enhance`, `/refactor verify`, or `/refactor complete`

**I will AUTOMATICALLY execute ALL these steps:**

1. **Deep Original Code Analysis**
   - Analyze EVERY function, method and class in detail
   - Document ALL behaviors, patterns and logic flows
   - Map complete code structure and dependencies
   - Create comprehensive understanding in `refactor/original-analysis.md`

2. **Complete Migration**
   - Apply ALL remaining refactorings
   - Find and fix ALL instances of old patterns
   - Update ALL imports and references
   - Clean up ALL orphaned code

   - Analyze refactored code line by line
   - Verify EVERY behavior is preserved
   - Check ALL logic paths match original
   - Ensure error handling is identical

4. **Comprehensive Analysis**
   - Line-by-line code comparison
   - Complexity metrics (before/after)
   - Performance benchmarks
   - Memory usage analysis
   - Test coverage verification

5. **Automatic Fixes**
   - Fix ANY behavioral discrepancies
   - Update broken references
   - Resolve type issues
   - Correct import paths

6. **Final Validation**
   - Run full test suite
   - Execute integration tests
   - Verify build passes
   - Ensure 100% behavior preservation

7. **Complete Report**
   - De-para mapping of ALL changes
   - Migration guide for team
   - Risk assessment
   - Rollback instructions if needed

**The result:** 100% guarantee that NOTHING was broken, NOTHING was left behind, and the application behaves EXACTLY the same as before refactoring.

   131→<implementation_rules>
   132→## Non-Negotiable Standards
   133→
   134→### 1. Naming (Clean & Searchable)
   135→```java
   136→// ❌ NEVER
   137→String d; // elapsed time
   138→List<String> l;
   139→Map<String, List<String>> grps;
   140→
   141→// ✅ ALWAYS
   142→String elapsedTimeInDays;
   143→List<String> locations;
   144→Map<String, List<String>> anagramGroups;
   145→```
   146→
   147→### 2. Function Rules
   148→- **< 20 lines** (no exceptions)
   149→- **Do ONE thing only**
   150→- **All parameters MUST be final**
   151→- **Max 3 parameters** (use objects for more)
   152→
   153→### 3. Import Rules
   154→```java
   155→import com.richemont.ce.domain.*;     // ❌ NEVER!
   156→import com.richemont.ce.domain.Order;  // ✅ ALWAYS!
   157→```
   158→
   159→### 4. Error Handling
   160→```java
   161→// ❌ NEVER hide failures
   162→try {
   163→    return repository.findById(id);
   164→} catch (Exception e) {
   165→    return null; // NO!
   166→}
   167→
   168→// ✅ Let it fail clearly
   169→return repository.findById(id)
   170→    .orElseThrow(() -> new NotFoundException(id));
   171→```
   172→
   173→### 5. Modern Java (21+) Usage
   174→- Records for DTOs
   175→- Pattern matching for clean switches
   176→- Text blocks for multi-line strings
   177→- Optional instead of null
   178→- Stream API for collections
   179→</implementation_rules>

   181→<effective_java_patterns>
   182→## Mandatory Patterns
   183→
   184→### 1. Static Factory Methods Over Constructors
   185→```java
   186→// ❌ BAD
   187→public User(String email) { }
   188→public User(String email, String name) { }
   189→
   190→// ✅ GOOD
   191→public static User withEmail(String email) { }
   192→public static User withEmailAndName(String email, String name) { }
   193→```
   194→
   195→### 2. Builder for Complex Objects (4+ parameters)
   196→```java
   197→@Builder(toBuilder = true)
   198→public class OrderRequest {
   199→    private final String customerId;
   200→    private final List<OrderItem> items;
   201→    @Builder.Default
   202→    private final ShippingMethod shipping = ShippingMethod.STANDARD;
   203→}
   204→```
   205→
   206→### 3. Favor Composition Over Inheritance
   207→```java
   208→// ❌ BAD - Deep hierarchy
   209→class Manager extends Employee { }
   210→
   211→// ✅ GOOD - Composition
   212→@Value
   213→public class Employee {
   214→    Person personalInfo;
   215→    Role role;
   216→}
   217→```
   218→
   219→### 4. Enums Instead of Constants
   220→```java
   221→// ❌ BAD
   222→public static final int STATUS_PENDING = 0;
   223→
   224→// ✅ GOOD
   225→public enum OrderStatus {
   226→    PENDING("Awaiting approval") {
   227→        @Override
   228→        public boolean canTransitionTo(OrderStatus status) {
   229→            return status == APPROVED || status == REJECTED;
   230→        }
   231→    }
   232→}
   233→```
   234→</effective_java_patterns>
